summary: Migrated the large fighter `ftdata` `.data` section out of assembly into C translation units, raising data-section coverage from 10.373322% to 11.929026% and one Event Match progress unit. The PR deleted most of `asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s` data, added `src/melee/ft/ftdata1.c`, `ftdata2.c`, and `ftdata3.c`, and moved adjacent data from `ft_unknown_006.s` into C files such as `ftwaitanim.c`, `ftitempickup.c`, `ftdynamics.c`, and `ftmario2.c`. It also expanded headers so the migrated C data could reference fighter callbacks, action-state tables, costume/demo string structs, and assembly jump-table labels.

key_files.role: Removed roughly 6900 lines of `.data` tables from assembly while leaving the remaining assembly code.
New C data unit for major fighter arrays including `ft_OnLoad`, `ft_OnDeath`, `ft_OnUserDataRemove`, `ActionStateTableByCharacter`, special-move callback arrays, and typed FTKIND_MAX-indexed data.
New C unit for ftdata assertion/error strings, including `ftData_assert_msg_0` through `ftData_assert_msg_5`, guarded with `MUST_MATCH` force-active pragmas.
New C unit for `ActionStateList[]` and large action-state data migrated from assembly.
Large header expansion with externs and prototypes needed for C references to migrated fighter data and callbacks.
Removed a `.data` block and marked branch-target labels global so C jump-table data could reference them.
Added forward typedefs for fighter-related callback/function pointer types such as `Fighter_ItemEvent`, `Fighter_ModelEvent`, and `Fighter_DemoGetter`.
Added the new C object files to the fighter object list, preserving build/link placement for matching.
Received migrated wait-animation strings and jump-table data such as `jtbl_803C54D8`.
Received migrated item-pickup assertion strings and jump tables such as `jtbl_803C55C0`, `jtbl_803C561C`, and `jtbl_803C5678`.
Received migrated fighter-dynamics strings and force-active data.
Many fighter headers gained externs/prototypes for on-load, on-death, special-action, action-state table, costume string, and demo string references.

what_changed: Deleted the bulk of the `.data` section from `asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s` and represented that data as C declarations/initializers.
Introduced three new ftdata C files: `ftdata1.c` for callback tables and fighter metadata arrays, `ftdata2.c` for assertion/error strings, and `ftdata3.c` for `ActionStateList[]`.

Moved several `.data` items formerly embedded near `ft_unknown_006.s` into dedicated C files: wait-animation strings/jump table, item-pickup strings/jump tables, dynamics strings/data, and Mario costume/demo file string data.
Updated `obj_files.mk` to build `ftdata1.c.o`, `ftdata2.c.o`, `ftdata3.c.o`, `ftwaitanim.c.o`, `ftunk07.c.o`, `ftitempickup.c.o`, `ftdynamics.c.o`, and `ftmario2.c.o` in the fighter object sequence.

Added `.global` directives for assembly labels such as `lbl_8008C388`, `lbl_8008C390`, `lbl_8008C398`, `lbl_8008C3A0`, `lbl_8008C3C4`, `lbl_80095728`, `lbl_80095738`, `lbl_8009588C`, `lbl_8009589C`, `lbl_80095990`, and `lbl_800959A0` so C jump-table objects can refer to those labels.
Added typed C arrays over `FTKIND_MAX`, including `HSD_GObjEvent` arrays, `ActionState*` tables, `Fighter_CostumeStrings*` arrays, and `Fighter_DemoStrings*` arrays.
Expanded forward declarations and character headers to make the migrated data compile without relying on raw assembly symbol references.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `#pragma force_active on` around selected string data to retain required objects for matching.
smart_moves: Split the enormous data migration into multiple C translation units instead of one monolith, making linker placement and future review more tractable.
Preserved unknown symbol names like `lbl_803C0FC8`, `lbl_803C57D0`, and `lbl_803C5A28` where semantic names were not yet justified, avoiding speculative renames.

Typed formerly raw `.4byte` tables as meaningful C arrays, for example `HSD_GObjEvent ft_OnLoad[FTKIND_MAX]`, `ActionState* ActionStateTableByCharacter[FTKIND_MAX]`, and `Fighter_DemoStrings`/`Fighter_CostumeStrings` structures.
Made assembly branch labels global only where the C data needed to reference them, a minimal bridge between remaining assembly and migrated C data.
Kept forced-active pragmas localized to the specific string data that needed retention, rather than applying broad linker hacks.
Used `FTKIND_MAX` array sizing to document that the callback and metadata tables are indexed by fighter kind.
Added many precise header externs/prototypes so the data migration improved type information rather than just relocating bytes.
decomp_lessons: Large `.data` sections can often be advanced by translating raw `.4byte` tables into typed C arrays before the surrounding code is fully decompiled.
When C data references labels still defined in assembly, the assembly labels must be exported with `.global`; otherwise jump tables and pointer tables will not link.
Object-file order in `obj_files.mk` is part of matching work for data migrations; new C objects need to be inserted at the correct point relative to remaining assembly objects.
Avoid over-naming unknown data during bulk migration. Keeping address-derived `lbl_` names is acceptable until later code/data analysis supports semantic names.
For string-only data that the compiler/linker might discard or reorder under matching builds, `MUST_MATCH` plus `#pragma force_active on` can preserve required output.
Forward typedefs for callback signatures reduce header churn and make large pointer tables readable without fully defining every struct.
FTKIND-indexed tables should be expressed with `FTKIND_MAX` where possible, because it documents table cardinality and helps catch missing entries.
Moving `.data` to C may require widespread but shallow header work across fighter-specific modules because the data table touches nearly every character.
naming_conventions: Unknown migrated globals retained address-style labels such as `lbl_803C0FC8`, `lbl_803C10D0`, `lbl_803C2150`, and `lbl_803C2468`.
Semantically known fighter dispatch tables used names such as `ft_OnLoad`, `ft_OnDeath`, `ft_OnUserDataRemove`, `ft_SpecialS`, `ft_SpecialAirHi`, `ft_SpecialAirLw`, and `ActionStateTableByCharacter`.
Assertion strings in `ftdata2.c` used the prefix `ftData_assert_msg_` with numeric suffixes.
Temporary struct names used the `ftData_Unk...Struct` pattern when field semantics were uncertain, for example `ftData_UnkCountStruct` and `ftData_UnkModelStruct`.
Jump-table globals kept the `jtbl_803C....` address-derived convention.
Fighter string/data structs used existing type names such as `Fighter_CostumeStrings` and `Fighter_DemoStrings` rather than raw pointer arrays.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed raw `.data` blocks from assembly files while leaving remaining code and required labels intact.
Added `.global` on assembly labels that became C data targets in jump tables.
Represented assembly jump tables as C `jtbl_t` objects in files like `ftwaitanim.c` and `ftitempickup.c`.
Used `#pragma force_active on` under `MUST_MATCH` to keep required strings emitted.
Inserted new C object files into `obj_files.mk` at the appropriate place near `ftdata.s` and `ft_unknown_006.s` to preserve link ordering.
Converted raw `.4byte NULL`/function-pointer sequences into typed arrays, improving C type checking while matching the original pointer layout.
Used address-label externs for data whose semantics were still unresolved, minimizing semantic risk during a bulk match migration.

review_feedback.note: No written review feedback or inline review comments were present in the PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftData_UnkCountStruct`, `ftData_UnkModelStruct`, or the `lbl_803C...` ftdata globals to semantic names.
Check whether later ftdata migrations continued the `ftdata1.c`/`ftdata2.c`/`ftdata3.c` split or consolidated these files.
Look for subsequent PRs that decompile the remaining code in `asm/melee/ft/ftdata.s` or `asm/melee/ft/ft_unknown_006.s`.
Search for later header cleanups that reduce the broad extern/prototype additions made to character headers in this PR.
Compare PR #717 and PR #718 to understand the staged ftdata migration strategy.
